wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Automated Academic Manuscript Formatter project. Cover initial setup instructions for both backend and frontend components, environment variable configuration, development server startup procedures, and basic usage examples. Include prerequisite requirements (Python 3.12, Node.js 18+, Docker), step-by-step installation guides, and verification steps to ensure proper setup. Document common setup issues and their solutions. Provide quick start examples for uploading documents, generating formatted outputs, and using the AI agent features. Include troubleshooting guidance for initial configuration problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Layers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the system layers architecture of the Automated Academic Manuscript Formatter. Detail the five-layer system design from browser to external services, including the Next.js 14 frontend layer with 34 routes, FastAPI-only backend gateway, pipeline processing layer, data layer with PostgreSQL, Redis, and ChromaDB, and external services layer. Explain the execution order of middleware stack (rate_limit → tier_rate_limit → jwks_verifier → abuse_detector → request_id → security_headers → prometheus_metrics), routing patterns, and how each layer interacts with adjacent layers. Include component responsibilities, data flow between layers, and security boundaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Next.js Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Next.js 14 application structure. Explain the App Router configuration, page organization patterns, and routing architecture. Document the root layout setup, metadata configuration, viewport settings, and font loading strategies. Cover the global CSS setup, theme configuration, and accessibility features like skip links. Include TypeScript configuration, ESLint setup, and build optimization settings. Address the client-side provider pattern, hydration handling, and SSR/SSG considerations. Provide guidelines for maintaining the application structure and adding new pages.

wiki_catalogs.name: FastAPI Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FastAPI application structure and initialization. Cover the main application setup, configuration management through environment variables, middleware stack configuration including CORS, rate limiting, security headers, and request ID tracking. Document the lifespan context manager for startup/shutdown procedures, dependency injection patterns, and router organization. Explain the modular routing system with v1 API versioning, authentication routes, document processing routes, and specialized endpoints. Include logging configuration, error tracking with Sentry, and health/readiness probes. Address development vs production configurations and security considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Document Processing Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for document processing endpoints. Document all HTTP methods (POST /api/v1/documents/, GET /api/v1/documents/{id}, PUT /api/v1/documents/{id}, DELETE /api/v1/documents/{id}) for document upload, retrieval, update, and deletion. Include detailed request schemas for file uploads, processing options, and metadata. Document response formats for document status, processing progress, and results. Cover authentication requirements, file format restrictions, size limits, and validation rules. Provide practical examples for uploading different document types (DOCX, PDF, TXT, MD), handling multipart form data, and processing large files. Include error handling for invalid formats, processing failures, and rate limiting scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the template architecture system. Explain the template contract-based design, directory structure organization, and template loading mechanisms. Document the relationship between template YAML contracts, CSL stylesheets, and rendering engines. Cover the template inheritance patterns, asset management system, and template discovery mechanisms. Include detailed explanations of template metadata, validation rules, and compatibility matrices. Provide examples of template structure layouts and explain how templates integrate with the formatting pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity model documentation covering all database entities including Document, Block, Reference, User, DocumentVersion, and ProcessingStatus models. Document each entity's fields, data types, constraints, and relationships. Explain the purpose and usage of each model in the document processing pipeline. Include field descriptions, validation rules, and business logic constraints. Document model inheritance patterns, composite keys, and unique constraints. Provide examples of model instantiation, querying patterns, and relationship navigation. Address model versioning, backward compatibility, and migration considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Orchestrator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Pipeline Orchestrator component. Explain the orchestrator's role as the central coordinator managing all 12+ pipeline stages. Document the sequential execution flow, stage coordination mechanisms, and error handling strategies. Cover the semaphore-based concurrency control, job scheduling, and resource management. Detail the status update system using Supabase and Redis pub/sub for real-time feedback. Include the safety mechanisms, timeout handling, and fallback strategies. Document the runtime flag resolution system for fast mode and semantic processing options. Provide examples of pipeline execution, debugging techniques, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: NVIDIA NIM Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the NVIDIA NIM integration system. Document the NVIDIA NIM client implementation, API configuration, and model deployment strategies. Explain the fallback mechanisms between NVIDIA NIM and other LLM providers like Groq and Ollama. Cover authentication setup, rate limiting, and cost optimization techniques. Include configuration examples for different NVIDIA NIM endpoints, error handling strategies, and performance monitoring. Document the integration with the main LLM service and how it fits into the overall AI pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the reusable UI component library. Document each component including Button, Input, Card, Badge, Skeleton with their props, styling variants, and usage patterns. Explain component composition, TailwindCSS integration, and theme customization options. Include accessibility features, keyboard navigation, and responsive design patterns. Provide code examples demonstrating different states, sizes, and configurations. Document component testing approaches and integration with form validation systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive infrastructure setup documentation covering containerization with Docker, local development environment configuration, and cloud infrastructure provisioning. Document the Docker Compose configuration for local development including service dependencies, port mappings, and environment variable configuration. Explain the recommended free-tier stack with Vercel, Render, Supabase, Upstash, and ChromaDB. Cover infrastructure requirements, resource constraints, and alternative hosting options. Include step-by-step setup procedures for development and production environments, dependency management, and service orchestration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend testing documentation covering pytest-based unit and integration testing. Document the test organization structure, marker-based test categorization (unit, integration, llm, service, contract), and execution strategies. Explain the test configuration in pytest.ini and pyproject.toml, including asyncio_mode settings and test markers. Cover the 46 backend test files including unit tests (~20), integration tests (~15), golden file tests, security tests, and SciBERT benchmark tests. Document test fixtures, mocking strategies for external services, and database testing approaches. Include guidelines for writing new backend tests, debugging test failures, and maintaining test reliability across different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the frontend service layer architecture. Document the core API service patterns, request/response handling mechanisms, and service class organization. Explain the fetchWithAuth wrapper, payload sanitization, and error handling strategies. Cover service initialization, configuration options, and dependency injection patterns. Include examples of service method implementations, parameter validation, and response transformation. Document caching strategies, retry mechanisms, and offline handling approaches. Address service composition patterns and integration with React Query for state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for WebSocket implementation in the real-time features. Document the ReconnectingWebSocket class implementation, connection lifecycle management, and automatic reconnection strategies. Explain the WebSocket URL construction, protocol conversion from HTTP to WS, and session-based routing. Cover connection state management including isConnected, isReconnecting flags and reconnectAttempt tracking. Detail the message handling system, payload structure, and event callbacks (onopen, onmessage, onclose, onerror, onreconnect). Include error handling patterns, connection timeout strategies, and graceful degradation when WebSocket is unavailable. Provide examples of establishing connections, sending messages, and handling disconnections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Context Providers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for all context providers in the application. Document AuthContext for authentication state management including user session handling, login/signup flows, and Supabase integration. Explain DocumentContext for document processing state, including job tracking, processing status, and document metadata. Cover ThemeContext for theme switching and user preference persistence. Detail ToastContext for notification management and user feedback systems. Include UserPreferencesContext for storing user settings and application preferences. Provide implementation examples, usage patterns, and best practices for each context provider. Address state persistence strategies and error handling approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vitest Unit & Component Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Vitest testing documentation for React components and hooks. Document the Vitest configuration in vitest.config.js including aliases, JSX automatic transformation, jsdom environment setup, and test file patterns. Explain the testing architecture with jsdom environment for DOM manipulation testing. Cover the current test suite of 12 test files focusing on critical components like AppShell, AuthGuard, ErrorBoundary, and StatusBadge. Document testing setup procedures, mock configurations for Next.js navigation, and best practices for React component testing. Include guidelines for writing effective unit tests for React hooks using useGeneratorState.test.js as an example. Address testing library requirements, debugging techniques, and test execution commands.

wiki_catalogs.name: Manual Testing Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive manual testing overview documentation explaining the overall manual testing strategy and procedures. Document the manual testing philosophy, testing objectives, and quality assurance approach. Explain the testing command structure, execution procedures, and test environment setup. Cover the relationship between automated and manual testing, when manual testing is preferred, and the role of manual verification in the quality assurance process. Include guidelines for systematic manual testing execution, test case documentation standards, and regression testing procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Configuration & Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive test configuration documentation covering pytest setup and backend testing infrastructure. Document the pytest.ini configuration including asyncio_mode settings, test collection patterns, and plugin configurations. Explain the pyproject.toml test settings and how they integrate with pytest. Detail the conftest.py configuration including global fixtures, database setup, and environment variable management. Cover test discovery patterns, marker-based test categorization, and test isolation strategies. Include setup instructions for local development, CI/CD test execution, and debugging test failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Phase 1 Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Phase 1 visual testing procedures focusing on early-stage document processing validation. Document the nine key visual verification scripts including parse and structure validation (01_parse_and_structure.py), equation visual verification (01b_equations_visual.py), classification accuracy assessment (02_classification.py), figure and table processing validation (03_figures_tables.py), reference detection verification (04_references.py), full pipeline execution (05_full_pipeline.py), figure numbering validation (05b_figure_numbering.py), figure insertion verification (05c_figure_insertion.py), formatted output validation (06_formatted.py), table numbering validation (06b_table_numbering.py), table insertion verification (06c_table_insertion.py), and equation visual verification (06d_equations_visual.py). Explain the sequential testing workflow, expected visual outcomes, quality criteria, and anomaly detection procedures for each script.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive architectural documentation for the Automated Academic Manuscript Formatter system. Describe the high-level system design, including the full-stack architecture with Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, and supporting technologies. Document the technology stack, component interactions, data flows, and integration patterns. Explain the pipeline-based processing architecture, real-time communication systems, and service dependencies. Include system context diagrams showing relationships between frontend, backend, external AI services, and databases. Address scalability considerations, deployment topology, and cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Request Flows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for all major request flows in the system architecture. Document the three primary user workflows: Formatter Mode A (Upload & Format) with background task processing, Formatter Mode B (Live Preview) with WebSocket communication, and Generator Modes (Multi-Doc Synthesis and AI Agent) with streaming responses. For each flow, describe the complete request/response cycle, including authentication, validation, background processing, real-time updates via Redis pub/sub, and final response delivery. Include timing constraints, error handling patterns, and state management across the entire request lifecycle.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the component architecture and UI component library. Document the reusable UI components (Button, Input, Card, Badge), layout components (AppShell, Header, Sidebar), and specialized components (FileUpload, Stepper, StatusBadge). Explain component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and styling approaches with TailwindCSS. Cover form components, validation patterns, and accessibility implementations. Include component state management, event handling, and integration with the broader application. Address component testing strategies and reusability patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware & Security System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the middleware and security system. Document the request ID tracking middleware for distributed tracing, rate limiting mechanisms including global and tier-based limits, security headers implementation with CSP and HSTS, abuse detection algorithms, and role-based access control (RBAC). Explain middleware execution order, configuration options, and integration patterns. Include security best practices, threat mitigation strategies, and monitoring approaches. Address performance implications and customization options for different deployment scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Management Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for template management endpoints. Document GET /api/v1/templates/ for listing available templates, GET /api/v1/templates/{id} for retrieving template details, POST /api/v1/templates/ for creating new templates, and PUT /api/v1/templates/{id} for updating existing templates. Include request/response schemas for template metadata, CSL styles, and formatting rules. Document template categories (academic styles like IEEE, APA, MLA), customization options, and publishing workflows. Cover authentication requirements for template access, permission levels for template modification, and validation rules for template creation. Provide examples for template creation, style switching, and template inheritance patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Templates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the 17+ academic templates supported by the system. Document each template's specific formatting rules, citation styles, and structural requirements. Cover IEEE, APA, MLA, Nature, Chicago, Springer, Elsevier, ACM, Harvard, Vancouver, and other specialized templates. Explain template-specific features like figure numbering, table formatting, equation handling, and reference management. Include examples of formatted outputs, template customization options, and publisher-specific compliance requirements. Document template selection criteria and automatic template detection mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed database schema documentation covering the complete database structure, table definitions, and column specifications. Document all tables, their columns, data types, constraints, and indexes. Explain the baseline schema and subsequent schema changes through migration files. Cover table relationships, foreign key constraints, and referential integrity rules. Document indexing strategies, performance considerations, and query optimization patterns. Include schema diagrams showing entity relationships and data flow. Address schema versioning, migration dependencies, and rollback procedures. Provide examples of complex queries and join patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Input Conversion
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the input conversion system. Explain the multi-format input handling including DOCX, PDF, TXT, MD, LaTeX, HTML, and other supported formats. Document the conversion pipeline from various input formats to internal document representation. Cover the DOCX conversion process, format detection algorithms, and fallback mechanisms. Detail the integration with external conversion services and local processing libraries. Include error handling for corrupted files, unsupported formats, and conversion failures. Provide examples of supported input formats, conversion workflows, and troubleshooting guides for common conversion issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Groq Fallback System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Groq fallback system within the AI/ML pipeline. Explain the fallback architecture that switches between NVIDIA NIM and Groq based on availability and performance. Document the configuration parameters, timeout handling, and error recovery mechanisms. Cover the integration patterns with the main LLM service and how fallback decisions are made. Include monitoring and logging strategies for fallback events, performance comparison metrics, and cost optimization through provider switching.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for layout and structural components. Document AppShell as the main container, Header with navigation and authentication controls, Sidebar for main navigation, and AuthGuard for route protection. Explain component hierarchy, prop interfaces, and integration patterns. Cover responsive design implementations, mobile navigation patterns, and accessibility compliance. Include examples of layout customization, theme integration, and state management coordination.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed environment configuration documentation covering all environment variables, secrets management, and configuration patterns. Document backend environment variables including Supabase credentials, LLM provider keys, Redis configuration, PDF parsing settings, and security parameters. Cover frontend environment variables with NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix requirements and deployment-specific configurations. Explain secrets management strategies, environment-specific overrides, and configuration validation procedures. Include security considerations for sensitive data handling and best practices for environment variable management across different deployment stages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend testing documentation covering Vitest unit/component testing and Playwright end-to-end testing. Document the test setup including vitest.config.js and playwright.config.js configurations. Explain the testing architecture with Vitest for React hooks and component isolation, and Playwright for browser-based E2E testing. Cover the current state of 93 test files where most are stubs needing real DOM assertions. Document the critical test paths prioritized for deployment validation. Include guidelines for writing effective React component tests, hook tests, and E2E browser automation tests. Address testing library requirements like @testing-library/dom and debugging E2E test failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for authentication service implementation and user management. Document the authentication flow including signup, login, password reset, and OTP verification processes. Explain Supabase integration patterns, OAuth authentication with Google, and redirect path sanitization. Cover token management, session handling, and automatic refresh mechanisms. Include error handling for authentication failures, account verification workflows, and security considerations. Document the AuthContext provider implementation and authentication state management throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Live Preview System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the live preview system implementation. Document the useLivePreviewSocket hook functionality, state management for HTML content, latency tracking, and warning display. Explain the debounced content sending mechanism, checksum calculation for content validation, and sequence number tracking. Cover the split editor interface with real-time preview pane, content synchronization, and cursor position handling. Detail the optimistic rendering approach, content diff detection, and performance optimization techniques. Include latency measurement implementation, warning system integration, and user feedback mechanisms. Provide guidelines for extending the preview system and handling edge cases like large content updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Query Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for React Query integration and server state management. Document query client configuration, caching strategies, and data synchronization patterns. Explain document-related queries for fetching document lists, processing status, and metadata. Cover generation session queries for real-time AI processing updates. Detail mutation patterns for document uploads, processing initiation, and state updates. Include error handling strategies, loading states management, and optimistic updates. Provide examples of query invalidation, refetching strategies, and cache persistence. Address performance optimization techniques and debugging approaches for server state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Playwright End-to-End Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed Playwright E2E testing documentation covering browser automation and user workflow testing. Document the Playwright configuration and setup for comprehensive testing of the Next.js application. Explain the extensive test suite of 45 test files covering critical user flows including authentication, document formatting, generator functionality, and template management. Cover test categories including authentication flows, dashboard interactions, formatter workflows, generator features, and responsive design testing. Document testing patterns for form submissions, file uploads, real-time features, and multi-step user journeys. Include guidelines for writing robust E2E tests, handling asynchronous operations, managing test data, and debugging test failures. Address browser compatibility testing, responsive design validation, and performance testing scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Visual Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed visual testing documentation covering the visual verification procedures and output validation processes. Document the three-phase visual testing approach (phase1, phase2, phase3) and their specific focus areas including parse and structure validation, classification verification, figure and table processing, and formatted output validation. Explain the visual output generation process, expected visual outcomes, and quality criteria for each phase. Cover the visual testing scripts, output comparison procedures, and manual verification workflows. Include guidelines for interpreting visual results, identifying anomalies, and documenting visual test outcomes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed unit testing documentation for backend pure logic components. Document testing strategies for individual functions, classes, and modules without external dependencies. Explain mocking approaches for database operations, external API calls, and file system interactions. Cover test case design patterns, assertion strategies, and edge case coverage. Include guidelines for testing FastAPI route handlers, Pydantic models, utility functions, and business logic components. Document test naming conventions, parameterized testing, and test data management. Provide examples of effective unit tests and common pitfalls to avoid.

wiki_catalogs.name: Phase 2 Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Phase 2 visual testing procedures focusing on intermediate-stage document processing validation. Document the two key visual verification scripts including classification verification (02_classification.py) and full pipeline execution (05_full_pipeline.py). Explain the phase transition validation criteria, processing stage integration verification, and quality assurance procedures. Cover the expected visual outcomes, validation workflows, and comparison procedures against baseline results. Include guidelines for identifying processing stage failures, validation thresholds, and corrective action procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend development documentation for the FastAPI application. Cover the application structure, middleware configuration, routing organization, and core business logic. Document the 12-stage pipeline processing system, including input conversion, parsing, structure detection, AI analysis, validation, formatting, and export stages. Explain the service layer architecture, database design with SQLAlchemy ORM, authentication and authorization systems, and real-time communication via Redis pub/sub. Include development workflows, testing strategies, and deployment considerations. Address performance optimization, error handling, and security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FastAPI middleware stack architecture. Detail the execution order and purpose of each middleware component: Prometheus metrics collection, base rate limiting, tier-aware rate limiting, abuse detection, request ID tracking, security headers (CSP, HSTS), and RBAC implementation. Explain how middleware components interact, their configuration options, performance impact, and security benefits. Include middleware ordering rationale, failure handling, and debugging techniques for middleware-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for state management patterns and data flow. Document the context providers (AuthContext, DocumentContext, ThemeContext, ToastContext), React Query integration for server state, and local state management with useState/useReducer. Explain authentication state handling, document processing state, user preferences, and notification systems. Cover custom hooks for API integration, real-time data streams, and session management. Include state persistence strategies, error boundary implementation, and performance optimization techniques. Address state synchronization across components and debugging approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Processing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the 12-stage pipeline processing system. Explain the PipelineOrchestrator design pattern, stage coordination mechanisms, and data flow between processing stages. Document the base pipeline architecture, common interfaces, and error handling strategies. Cover the modular stage design allowing for extensibility, stage dependencies, and conditional execution logic. Include performance monitoring, stage timeout handling, and recovery mechanisms. Address pipeline configuration, debugging techniques, and optimization strategies for large-scale document processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Document Generator Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for AI document generation endpoints. Document POST /api/v1/generator/session for creating generation sessions, GET /api/v1/generator/session/{id} for session status, and POST /api/v1/generator/session/{id}/cancel for canceling generation. Include streaming endpoints for real-time content delivery via Server-Sent Events. Document request schemas for generation parameters (topic, length, style, template), session management, and content approval workflows. Cover authentication requirements, token limits, and rate limiting for AI generation. Provide examples for outline approval, content iteration, multi-document generation, and streaming response handling. Include error handling for generation failures, content filtering, and session timeouts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Creation Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive template creation documentation covering the complete development workflow. Document the template development environment setup, contract definition syntax, and CSL integration process. Explain conditional logic implementation, dynamic content insertion, and template validation procedures. Cover template testing methodologies, debugging techniques, and quality assurance processes. Include step-by-step tutorials for creating custom templates, modifying existing templates, and integrating new citation styles. Document template packaging, distribution, and maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: ORM Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive ORM configuration documentation covering SQLAlchemy setup, session management, and database connection handling. Document the declarative base configuration, session factory setup, and connection pooling strategies. Explain the Supabase integration, remote database configuration, and local development setup. Cover transaction management, connection lifecycle, and error handling patterns. Document database initialization, engine configuration, and environment-specific settings. Include examples of ORM usage patterns, query construction, and bulk operations. Address performance optimization techniques, connection reuse, and resource cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Text Extraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the text extraction system. Document the ParserFactory pattern for handling different file formats including PDF, DOCX, TXT, HTML, Markdown, LaTeX, and specialized parsers. Explain the extraction algorithms for each format type, OCR fallback mechanisms for scanned PDFs using Nougat, and metadata extraction processes. Cover the block-based document structure, text normalization, and layout preservation. Detail the integration with external services like GROBID and Docling for enhanced extraction. Include performance optimization techniques, error handling strategies, and debugging approaches for extraction failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Ollama Local Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Ollama local deployment system. Document the local model serving setup, model management, and resource allocation strategies. Explain the integration with the LLM service for local inference scenarios. Cover model loading, caching mechanisms, and performance tuning for local deployments. Include configuration examples for different model sizes, GPU utilization, and memory optimization. Document troubleshooting common local deployment issues and monitoring local model performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for form and input components. Document FileUpload with drag-and-drop functionality, CategoryTabs for document type selection, FormattingOptions for processing preferences, and ProcessingStepper for multi-step workflows. Explain form validation patterns, error handling, and user feedback mechanisms. Cover component state management, async operations, and integration with backend APIs. Include accessibility features, internationalization support, and responsive design considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: CI/CD Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CI/CD pipeline documentation covering automated testing, deployment workflows, and release management. Document the GitHub Actions workflows for backend and frontend including build processes, testing phases, and deployment triggers. Explain the multi-stage deployment pipeline from development to production, including quality gates, security scanning, and rollback procedures. Cover branch protection rules, code review requirements, and automated deployment strategies. Include troubleshooting common CI/CD issues, performance optimization, and monitoring deployment success rates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Manual Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive manual testing documentation covering the manual testing procedures and workflows used for quality assurance. Document the manual testing directory structure including visual testing procedures, interface verification, and specialized test scripts. Explain the manual testing phases (phase1, phase2, phase3) and their specific focus areas including caption matching, figure detection, table extraction, and full pipeline validation. Cover the visual output generation and verification processes. Document the manual testing commands, test scripts for equation verification, HITL (Human-in-the-loop) verification, and RAG interface testing. Include guidelines for systematic manual testing, test case documentation, and regression testing procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Schema Validation & Type Safety
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for schema validation implementation using Zod. Document the schema definitions for API responses, request payloads, and form data validation. Explain validation patterns, error handling strategies, and type inference benefits. Cover schema composition, conditional validation rules, and custom validation functions. Include examples of form validation, API response parsing, and error message formatting. Document testing strategies for schema validation and integration with TypeScript for enhanced type safety.

wiki_catalogs.name: Status Updates & Progress Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for status updates and progress tracking implementation. Document the job status card component, status state management, and real-time progress indicators. Explain the status constants definition, status transition logic, and progress percentage calculation. Cover the backend event system for status updates, job completion tracking, and error state propagation. Detail the optimistic UI updates, status polling fallback mechanisms, and user notification systems. Include examples of status display components, progress bar implementations, and error handling patterns. Address performance considerations for frequent status updates and memory management for long-running jobs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Hooks
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for custom hooks and reusable logic patterns. Document useGeneratorSessionStream for real-time AI generation updates and WebSocket communication. Explain useSessionEventStream for processing pipeline status updates. Cover useUpload for file upload handling, progress tracking, and error management. Detail useGeneratorState for managing AI document generation state and workflow. Include useLivePreviewSocket for real-time preview updates and useAutosave for automatic saving mechanisms. Document useUnsavedChanges for detecting and handling unsaved modifications. Provide implementation examples, hook composition patterns, and integration guidelines. Address performance considerations and debugging approaches for custom hooks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Specialized Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create specialized testing documentation covering domain-specific verification procedures and tools. Document the equation verification testing process, Human-in-the-loop (HITL) JATS verification, RAG interface testing, and interface validation procedures. Include guidelines for running specialized tests, interpreting results, and addressing domain-specific issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive integration testing documentation covering end-to-end testing with external services and real-world scenarios. Document the integration test suite including Crossref API integration, CSL formatting integration, Docling document processing integration, and template rendering validation. Explain test environment setup, service mocking strategies, and data fixture management. Cover testing approaches for database interactions, external API calls, and multi-service workflows. Include guidelines for writing reliable integration tests, handling flaky tests, and managing test data. Document test isolation, cleanup procedures, and performance considerations for integration test suites.

wiki_catalogs.name: Phase 3 Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Phase 3 visual testing procedures focusing on final output validation and quality assurance. Document the formatted output validation script (06_formatted.py) and its role in the final verification stage. Explain the final quality assessment criteria, output formatting validation, and completeness verification procedures. Cover the expected final formatted document characteristics, validation workflows, and acceptance criteria. Include guidelines for final review procedures, quality scoring systems, and documentation requirements for completed visual tests.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend development documentation for the Next.js 14 application. Cover the App Router structure, page organization, component architecture, and state management patterns. Document the UI component library, form handling, real-time features implementation, and API integration strategies. Explain the authentication flow, context providers, React Query integration, and error boundary handling. Include component composition patterns, styling approaches with TailwindCSS, and responsive design considerations. Address performance optimization techniques, accessibility compliance, and cross-browser compatibility. Provide guidelines for extending the UI and adding new features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the data flow architecture throughout the system. Document the complete data journey from user upload through the 12-stage pipeline to final export, including data transformation at each stage, caching strategies using Redis, database persistence patterns, and real-time data streaming via WebSocket and SSE. Explain data validation points, error propagation, state management across asynchronous operations, and how data flows between internal components and external services. Include performance considerations and data consistency guarantees.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for API integration patterns and service layer implementation. Document the service classes for authentication, document operations, generation sessions, and template management. Explain request/response handling, error management, loading states, and authentication integration. Cover schema validation with Zod, analytics integration with PostHog, and performance monitoring. Include API client configuration, caching strategies, and offline handling. Address authentication token management, request interceptors, and response transformation patterns. Provide guidelines for extending API services and adding new endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the service layer architecture and business logic implementation. Document the service abstraction pattern, dependency injection mechanisms, and service lifecycle management. Cover core services including document processing, authentication, LLM integration, enhancement management, and external API clients. Explain service interfaces, error handling patterns, transaction management, and caching strategies. Include service configuration, testing approaches, and integration with the pipeline system. Address service isolation, fault tolerance, and monitoring requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Document Synthesis Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for multi-document synthesis endpoints. Document POST /api/v1/synthesis/ for initiating document synthesis, GET /api/v1/synthesis/{id} for synthesis status, and POST /api/v1/synthesis/{id}/cancel for canceling synthesis operations. Include request schemas for document alignment, conflict resolution, citation assembly, and output formatting. Document synthesis algorithms, content matching strategies, and template-based merging processes. Cover authentication requirements, document size limitations, and processing constraints. Provide examples for intelligent content alignment, citation harmonization, and multi-source document integration. Include error handling for conflicting content, missing references, and synthesis failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: CSL Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Citation Style Language (CSL) integration system. Explain how CSL stylesheets are loaded, processed, and applied during template rendering. Document the CSL engine architecture, style resolution mechanisms, and citation formatting algorithms. Cover the integration with external CSL repositories, style caching strategies, and fallback mechanisms. Include examples of CSL customization, style modification workflows, and troubleshooting CSL-related issues. Address CSL version compatibility and style validation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed migration system documentation covering Alembic configuration, migration generation, and schema evolution management. Document the migration environment setup, target metadata configuration, and revision dependencies. Explain migration generation commands, autogenerate features, and manual migration creation. Cover migration execution strategies, downgrade procedures, and rollback mechanisms. Document best practices for writing migrations, handling data transformations, and maintaining backward compatibility. Include examples of complex migrations, data seeding, and schema restructuring. Address migration testing, environment-specific migrations, and production deployment procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Structure Detection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the structure detection system. Explain the heading identification algorithms, section boundary detection, and semantic structure analysis. Document the heading rule engine, position-based heuristics, and machine learning-based classification. Cover the integration with external AI services for enhanced detection accuracy. Detail the structure validation, confidence scoring, and error correction mechanisms. Include examples of detected structures, rule configuration, and troubleshooting guides for misclassified sections.

wiki_catalogs.name: SciBERT Classification
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the SciBERT classification system used in academic paper processing. Document the SciBERT model integration, paper classification algorithms, and domain-specific training data. Explain the classification pipeline, confidence scoring, and multi-label classification capabilities. Cover model training procedures, evaluation metrics, and performance optimization. Include examples of classification results, threshold tuning, and integration with the document processing pipeline. Document model versioning, retraining procedures, and quality assurance processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Specialized Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for specialized application-specific components. Document AI generator components (AgentChatPane, DocumentBuildPane, TokenStream), live preview components (PreviewPane, SplitEditor), dashboard components (DashboardRow, DashboardStats), and synthesis components (MultiUploadPanel, SynthesisStageTimeline). Explain complex state management, real-time data handling, and integration with WebSocket streams. Cover component lifecycle, performance optimization, and error recovery patterns. Include examples of component composition and advanced usage scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Alerting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed monitoring and alerting documentation covering system observability, performance metrics, and operational health monitoring. Document the Prometheus monitoring stack configuration, Grafana dashboard setup, and key performance indicators to track. Explain application-level metrics, infrastructure monitoring, and business metrics collection. Cover alerting strategies, threshold configurations, and notification channels. Include log aggregation, error tracking with Sentry, and real-time performance monitoring. Address capacity planning, performance optimization, and proactive issue detection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Infrastructure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed test infrastructure documentation covering the complete testing environment setup and CI/CD integration. Document the Docker Compose configuration for test services including Redis, database, and external service dependencies. Explain the GitHub Actions workflows for backend CI (pytest) and frontend CI (Vitest + build). Cover the test configuration files including pytest.ini, pyproject.toml, vitest.config.js, and playwright.config.js. Document the test data management, environment variable requirements, and service mocking strategies. Include guidelines for setting up local testing environments, configuring CI/CD pipelines, and managing test dependencies across different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics & Performance Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for analytics integration and performance monitoring systems. Document PostHog integration for event tracking, user behavior analytics, and feature usage metrics. Explain analytics event collection, custom property tracking, and user identification patterns. Cover performance monitoring with metrics collection, error tracking with Sentry, and real-time event streaming. Include guidelines for adding new analytics events, privacy considerations, and opt-out mechanisms. Document the integration with session event streams and real-time user interaction tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Streaming Responses
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for streaming response implementation. Document the backend streaming endpoints, SSE (Server-Sent Events) implementation, and real-time data transmission. Explain the frontend stream hooks for generator sessions and synthesis sessions, including connection establishment, message parsing, and state updates. Cover the streaming data format, chunk processing, and error recovery mechanisms. Detail the optimistic UI updates during streaming, partial content rendering, and completion handling. Include examples of implementing new streaming endpoints, handling connection interruptions, and managing stream lifecycle. Address performance optimization for large streams and memory management for extended sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for real-time state management and WebSocket integration. Document WebSocket connection handling, reconnection logic, and message streaming patterns. Explain real-time preview updates for document formatting and generation processes. Cover token streaming for AI-generated content and incremental updates. Detail state synchronization between client and server, including conflict resolution and optimistic updates. Include error handling for network interruptions, connection failures, and message queuing. Provide examples of real-time UI updates, progress tracking, and user feedback mechanisms. Address performance optimization for real-time data streams and debugging approaches for WebSocket communication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive pipeline validation documentation covering the end-to-end pipeline testing procedures and validation workflows. Document the manual pipeline testing scripts including database table verification, FITZ table validation, debug inspection procedures, and final system checks. Explain the pipeline validation criteria, integration point verification, and system-wide testing procedures. Cover the validation scripts' execution methods, expected outcomes, and troubleshooting procedures. Include guidelines for comprehensive pipeline testing, validation result interpretation, and system health verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contract Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed contract testing documentation for API endpoint validation and template asset integrity. Document endpoint contract testing including request/response schema validation, status code verification, and behavioral contract enforcement. Explain template asset integrity testing covering template file validation, marker checking, and regression testing. Cover contract-driven development approaches, golden file testing strategies, and automated contract verification. Include guidelines for maintaining contract consistency, handling breaking changes, and testing backward compatibility. Document contract testing tools, validation frameworks, and continuous integration approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Visual Output Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for visual output management procedures and quality control processes. Document the visual output storage system, file organization structure, and output comparison workflows. Explain the visual output generation process, storage conventions, and retrieval procedures. Cover the quality assessment criteria for visual outputs, comparison methodologies, and documentation requirements. Include guidelines for managing large volumes of visual test outputs, version control of visual results, and archival procedures for visual test evidence.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints and real-time communication protocols. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for all 34 routes. Include detailed parameter descriptions, validation rules, error codes, and response formats. Cover the document processing API, template management API, generator API, synthesis API, and utility endpoints. Document WebSocket connections for real-time updates, message formats, and event types. Provide practical examples for each endpoint, client implementation guidelines, and integration patterns. Include rate limiting information, versioning details, and backwards compatibility notes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation for the Automated Academic Manuscript Formatter. Detail the complete tech stack including Next.js 14 frontend framework, FastAPI backend with Uvicorn, PostgreSQL with Supabase, Redis caching, ChromaDB vector store, and external AI services (NVIDIA NIM, Groq, Ollama, GROBID, Docling). Document version requirements, integration patterns, dependency relationships, and rationale for technology choices. Include infrastructure requirements, deployment considerations, and upgrade paths for each technology component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for real-time features implementation. Document WebSocket integration for live previews, status updates, and streaming responses. Explain the reconnecting WebSocket implementation, message handling, and error recovery strategies. Cover the live preview system, real-time status indicators, and progress tracking components. Include event-driven UI updates, optimistic rendering patterns, and conflict resolution. Address WebSocket lifecycle management, connection state handling, and graceful degradation. Provide guidelines for implementing new real-time features and debugging connection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database & ORM Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the database design and SQLAlchemy ORM implementation. Document the entity relationship models including Document, Block, Reference, User, and processing status entities. Explain the declarative base configuration, relationship mappings, and inheritance patterns. Cover database session management, connection pooling, and transaction handling. Include Alembic migration system, schema evolution strategies, and data integrity constraints. Address performance optimization techniques, indexing strategies, and query optimization patterns. Document database configuration, connection management, and backup/recovery procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for real-time communication endpoints. Document WebSocket connections for live status updates, Server-Sent Events (SSE) for progressive document updates, and preview rendering endpoints. Include connection establishment procedures, message formats, event types (processing_started, progress_update, completion, error), and subscription management. Document preview endpoints for real-time document rendering, collaborative editing features, and live collaboration protocols. Cover connection authentication, heartbeat mechanisms, reconnection strategies, and error recovery. Provide examples for establishing WebSocket connections, handling real-time events, implementing client-side reconnection logic, and managing concurrent connections. Include performance considerations for high-concurrency scenarios and connection pooling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Rendering Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the template rendering engine and formatting pipeline. Explain the rendering process from template contract to final formatted output. Document the template execution engine, variable substitution mechanisms, and conditional rendering logic. Cover section ordering algorithms, numbering systems, and cross-reference resolution. Include detailed explanations of formatting rules application, style inheritance, and content transformation processes. Provide examples of rendering workflows and debugging techniques for formatting issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AI analysis components. Document the content analysis engine, semantic parsing, and classification systems. Explain the integration with external AI services including NVIDIA NIM, Groq, and Ollama. Cover the RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) engine implementation, reasoning engine capabilities, and SciBERT-based classification. Detail the confidence scoring, multi-modal analysis, and contextual understanding features. Include configuration options for different AI providers, performance optimization, and troubleshooting AI-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: RAG Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) engine implementation. Document the retrieval system architecture, embedding strategies, and cross-reference integration. Explain the document indexing process, similarity search algorithms, and relevance ranking. Cover the integration with Crossref for academic paper retrieval and the semantic search capabilities. Include configuration options for different embedding models, retrieval parameters, and performance tuning. Document the generation pipeline that combines retrieved context with LLM responses, error handling, and quality assessment of retrieved results.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for utility and helper components. Document confirmation dialogs, error boundaries, export functionality, feedback forms, health indicators, metrics displays, notifications, onboarding tours, previews, status badges, validation cards, upgrade modals, toggle switches, and empty states. Explain component reusability patterns, configuration options, and integration with global state management. Cover accessibility implementations, animation effects, and responsive behavior. Include examples of component customization and advanced usage patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security operations documentation covering security architecture, threat mitigation, and operational security procedures. Document authentication and authorization mechanisms, API security patterns, and access control implementations. Explain security middleware, rate limiting, abuse detection, and input validation strategies. Cover vulnerability management, penetration testing procedures, and security incident response protocols. Include compliance requirements, data protection measures, and security monitoring. Address secure deployment practices, secrets management, and continuous security assessment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing best practices documentation covering guidelines for writing, maintaining, and executing tests effectively. Document testing methodologies including test-driven development approaches, test organization principles, and code coverage strategies. Explain debugging techniques for test failures, test data management, and test maintenance procedures. Cover performance testing considerations, load testing approaches, and scalability testing for the document processing pipeline. Document test documentation standards, naming conventions, and reporting practices. Include guidelines for test automation, continuous integration testing, and maintaining test reliability across different deployment environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create test data management documentation covering the sample input handling, golden file validation, and test data organization procedures. Document the sample input directory structure, test file formats, and data preparation procedures. Explain the golden file system including input test files and expected output validation. Cover the test data lifecycle, version management, and regression testing data maintenance. Include guidelines for adding new test cases, updating golden files, and maintaining test data consistency across different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security testing documentation covering backend security validation and risk mitigation testing. Document chaos testing approaches, global safety validation, and security verification procedures. Explain testing strategies for input validation, authentication bypass attempts, and authorization checks. Cover vulnerability assessment testing, penetration testing methodologies, and security regression testing. Include guidelines for testing rate limiting, abuse detection, and input sanitization. Document security test automation, threat modeling validation, and compliance testing approaches. Provide examples of common security vulnerabilities and their testing methodologies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive template system documentation covering the complete template architecture and creation process. Document the 17+ academic templates (IEEE, APA, MLA, Nature, etc.), template contract system, and CSL integration. Explain the template rendering engine, conditional logic implementation, and dynamic content insertion. Cover the template creation workflow, testing procedures, and validation processes. Document template assets management, style customization options, and publisher-specific formatting rules. Include practical examples of template modification, custom template development, and troubleshooting template-related issues. Address template versioning, distribution, and maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for routing patterns and navigation implementation. Document the App Router structure with route groups, protected routes, and authentication guards. Explain the navigation sidebar, breadcrumb systems, and active state management. Cover dynamic routing, query parameter handling, and URL state synchronization. Include navigation guards, redirect logic, and route protection mechanisms. Address responsive navigation, mobile-first design patterns, and accessibility considerations. Provide guidelines for adding new routes, implementing nested layouts, and optimizing navigation performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for real-time communication systems including WebSocket implementation, Server-Sent Events (SSE), and Redis pub/sub integration. Document the event-driven architecture for live status updates, progress tracking, and collaborative features. Explain the WebSocket connection handling, message serialization, and client-server communication patterns. Cover Redis pub/sub implementation for scalable event distribution, caching strategies for real-time data, and connection management. Include error handling, reconnection logic, and performance optimization for high-concurrency scenarios. Address security considerations and rate limiting for real-time endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility and Administrative Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for utility and administrative endpoints. Document GET /api/health for system health checks, GET /api/metrics for performance metrics, POST /api/feedback for user feedback submission, and deprecation notices for API versioning. Include health check response formats, metric collection endpoints, feedback submission schemas, and API version compatibility information. Cover monitoring endpoints for system status, resource utilization tracking, and performance indicators. Document administrative endpoints for system maintenance, user management, and operational controls. Provide examples for health monitoring integration, metrics collection, feedback processing, and API version migration strategies. Include error handling for system unavailability, metric collection failures, and administrative access restrictions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the validation system. Explain the document validation engine, quality assurance processes, and automated review mechanisms. Document the validation rules, error detection algorithms, and confidence-based scoring. Cover the AI explainer system for validation decisions, review management workflows, and quality improvement suggestions. Detail the integration with external validation services and manual review processes. Include examples of validation reports, common validation errors, and remediation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reasoning Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the reasoning engine that handles complex document analysis and decision-making. Document the semantic parsing capabilities, logical reasoning workflows, and multi-step problem solving. Explain the integration with the semantic parser for structured content extraction and the decision-making algorithms. Cover the rule-based reasoning system, constraint satisfaction, and conflict resolution mechanisms. Include examples of reasoning workflows, debugging techniques, and performance optimization for complex reasoning tasks. Document the integration with other pipeline components and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Maintenance & Troubleshooting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive maintenance and troubleshooting documentation covering operational procedures, problem resolution, and system maintenance practices. Document routine maintenance tasks, database maintenance, cache management, and system cleanup procedures. Explain troubleshooting methodologies for common issues including PDF parsing failures, LLM provider errors, and infrastructure problems. Cover performance tuning, capacity planning, and scalability considerations. Include emergency procedures, disaster recovery plans, and system restoration processes. Address preventive maintenance, health checks, and operational best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Specialized Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop specialized testing documentation covering AI/ML model testing, performance benchmarking, and manual verification procedures. Document SciBERT model benchmark testing, DeepSeek model validation, and performance load testing strategies. Explain testing approaches for machine learning models, accuracy validation, and performance regression testing. Cover load testing with Locust, manual testing procedures, and verification scripts. Include guidelines for testing AI/ML pipelines, model versioning, and performance optimization. Document specialized testing tools, benchmark datasets, and validation metrics. Provide examples of testing AI/ML components and performance-critical backend services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed pipeline processing documentation covering the 12-stage document processing workflow. Document each pipeline stage including input conversion, text extraction, structure detection, AI analysis, validation, formatting, and export. Explain the pipeline orchestrator, stage coordination, error handling, and performance optimization. Cover the AI/ML integration points, external service interactions, and fallback mechanisms. Document the processing state management, job tracking, and real-time status updates. Include troubleshooting guides for pipeline failures, performance tuning, and debugging techniques. Address extensibility options for adding new pipeline stages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authentication and authorization system built on Supabase Auth. Document JWT token handling, JWKS verification, and session management. Explain the role-based access control (RBAC) implementation, permission checking mechanisms, and resource-level authorization. Cover user registration, login flows, password reset, and OTP verification processes. Include security middleware integration, token refresh strategies, and logout handling. Address multi-factor authentication, account security features, and audit logging for security events. Document integration with external identity providers and compliance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Formatting Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the formatting engine. Explain the template-based formatting system, style mapping, and academic format compliance. Document the template rendering engine, CSL (Citation Style Language) integration, and dynamic formatting rules. Cover the section ordering, numbering systems, and reference formatting. Detail the integration with publisher-specific templates and custom formatting requirements. Include examples of supported formats, template customization, and troubleshooting formatting issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the model management system that handles AI/ML model lifecycle. Document the model storage architecture, versioning strategies, and deployment automation. Explain the model registry, metadata management, and dependency tracking. Cover the integration with different AI providers, model validation procedures, and performance monitoring. Include configuration management for different environments, model caching strategies, and resource optimization. Document the model update procedures, rollback mechanisms, and quality assurance processes for AI models.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI/ML Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive AI/ML integration documentation covering the multiple AI/ML services and models used in the system. Document the NVIDIA NIM integration, Groq fallback system, local Ollama deployment, and SciBERT classification. Explain the model management system, caching strategies, and performance optimization. Cover the RAG engine implementation, embedding systems, and rule-based retrieval. Document the reasoning engine, semantic parsing, and validation systems. Include configuration options for different AI providers, cost optimization strategies, and fallback mechanisms. Address model versioning, monitoring, and troubleshooting AI-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Metrics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the monitoring and metrics system including Prometheus instrumentation, custom metrics collection, and Grafana dashboards. Document the metrics exposure endpoints, performance monitoring, and health check implementations. Explain custom metric definitions for pipeline performance, queue depths, processing times, and error rates. Cover alerting strategies, log aggregation, and distributed tracing integration. Include dashboard configurations, metric retention policies, and capacity planning insights. Address production monitoring best practices, troubleshooting workflows, and performance optimization based on metrics analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Export System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the export system. Explain the multi-format export capabilities including DOCX, PDF, LaTeX, JATS XML, and other academic formats. Document the export pipeline, format-specific processing, and quality assurance measures. Cover the integration with external export services, template-based exports, and batch processing capabilities. Detail the export configuration options, format validation, and troubleshooting export failures. Include examples of supported export formats, customization options, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database design documentation covering the entity relationship models, schema design, and data access patterns. Document all database entities including Document, Block, Reference, User, and processing status models. Explain the relationship between entities, foreign key constraints, and indexing strategies. Cover the SQLAlchemy ORM configuration, database session management, and transaction handling. Document the migration system, schema evolution, and data integrity measures. Include performance optimization techniques, query patterns, and caching strategies. Address data lifecycle management, backup procedures, and database maintenance tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the pipeline services integration. Explain the external service clients including Crossref, GROBID, Docling, and CSL engines. Document the service discovery, connection management, and error handling strategies. Cover the integration patterns, timeout management, and fallback mechanisms. Detail the service configuration, authentication, and performance optimization. Include examples of service usage, troubleshooting service connectivity issues, and monitoring service health.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation covering the complete testing strategy and implementation approach. Document unit testing procedures, integration testing with external services, end-to-end testing with Playwright, and manual testing workflows. Explain the test organization, fixture usage, and mocking strategies. Cover the continuous integration testing pipeline, performance testing, and load testing procedures. Document testing best practices, test data management, and automated testing workflows. Include guidelines for writing new tests, debugging test failures, and maintaining test reliability. Address testing in different environments and deployment scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Safety
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the pipeline safety mechanisms. Explain the circuit breaker patterns, LLM validation, retry guards, and safe execution wrappers. Document the error containment strategies, timeout handling, and graceful degradation mechanisms. Cover the safety validation rules, risk mitigation techniques, and failure recovery procedures. Detail the integration with monitoring systems and alerting mechanisms. Include examples of safety configurations, troubleshooting safety violations, and performance impact considerations.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation covering the complete deployment pipeline and operational procedures. wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation addressing common issues, debugging techniques, and frequently asked questions.
